chunk_summary: This section outlines the diagnostic and therapeutic pathway for suspected cow’s milk protein allergy (CMPA) in preterm, LBW, and ELBW infants post-discharge, emphasizing phenotype identification, elimination trials, and re-challenge protocols to confirm diagnosis while supporting breastfeeding and nutritional adequacy.
learning_objectives: Understand the diagnostic criteria and phenotypes of cow’s milk protein allergy (CMPA) in preterm and LBW infants.
Learn the appropriate use of elimination diets and re-challenge protocols for CMPA diagnosis.
Identify safe feeding practices and nutritional support strategies for breastfeeding and formula-fed infants with suspected CMPA.
Recognize alternative formula options and their indications, including extensively hydrolyzed formulas (EHF) and amino acid formulas (AAF).
key_facts: CMPA is often over-diagnosed in infants with regurgitation, colic, or constipation, leading to unnecessary interventions.
Immediate reactions (e.g., urticaria, wheeze, angioedema) suggest an IgE-mediated allergy, while delayed reactions (e.g., blood-streaked stools, eczema, vomiting) are more common in young infants.
For breastfeeding infants, maternal cow’s milk elimination with dietetic support is recommended, with symptom improvement expected in 2–4 weeks.
For formula-fed infants, extensively hydrolyzed formulas (EHF) are first-line, with amino acid formulas (AAF) reserved for severe cases or EHF failure.
Soy formula may be considered in older infants but carries a risk of cross-reactivity.
Diagnosis should be confirmed by reintroducing cow’s milk under clinician guidance, except in cases of severe prior reactions.
reference_quote: ‘A structured outpatient pathway reduces both missed cases and overtreatment.’ (Indian Pediatrics, 2020)
document_key_topics: Diagnostic criteria for CMPA in preterm and LBW infants
Therapeutic strategies for breastfeeding and formula-fed infants
Role of elimination diets and re-challenge in CMPA diagnosis
Nutritional adequacy and safe feeding practices
